questions.question: Which NBA team did Shaquille O'Neal win his first three championship rings with?
questions.answer: the Los Angeles Lakers

questions.question: Which NBA player holds the record for the most career triple-doubles in league history?
questions.answer: Russell Westbrook with 198

questions.question: Which NBA player was nicknamed 'The Mailman' because he always delivered, and won back-to-back MVP awards in 1988 and 1989?
questions.answer: Karl Malone of the Utah Jazz

questions.question: Which NBA team did LeBron James lead to a historic comeback from a 3-1 deficit to win the 2016 NBA Championship?
questions.answer: the Cleveland Cavaliers

questions.question: Which NBA team did Kevin Durant join in 2016, forming a superteam that went on to win back-to-back championships in 2017 and 2018?
questions.answer: the Golden State Warriors

questions.question: Which NBA player famously scored 81 points in a single game against the Toronto Raptors in January 2006, the second-highest single-game total in NBA history?
questions.answer: Kobe Bryant of the Los Angeles Lakers

questions.question: Which NBA player won the Defensive Player of the Year award four consecutive times between 2015 and 2018, the first player ever to achieve that feat?
questions.answer: Kawhi Leonard of the San Antonio Spurs

questions.question: Which NBA player won his only championship with the Dallas Mavericks in 2011, earning Finals MVP honors after averaging 26 points per game in the series against the Miami Heat?
questions.answer: Dirk Nowitzki of the Dallas Mavericks

questions.question: Which NBA player won his record fifth MVP award in 2012, making him the most decorated MVP in league history at that time?
questions.answer: Kareem Abdul-Jabbar of the Los Angeles Lakers

questions.question: Which NBA player became the first guard in league history to win the Defensive Player of the Year award, earning the honor in the 1987-88 season while playing for the Detroit Pistons?
questions.answer: Michael Jordan of the Chicago Bulls

questions.question: In the 1980 horror film 'The Shining,' what phrase does Jack Torrance repeatedly type throughout the manuscript he is writing?
questions.answer: All work and no play makes Jack a dull boy

questions.question: In the 1978 horror film 'Halloween,' what is the clinical term Dr. Loomis uses to describe Michael Myers, calling him the embodiment of pure evil?
questions.answer: the boogeyman

questions.question: In the 1991 horror film 'The Silence of the Lambs,' what specific instructions does Hannibal Lecter give Clarice about how she will know when Buffalo Bill's next victim has been found?
questions.answer: she will be found missing her skin

questions.question: In the 1984 horror film 'A Nightmare on Elm Street,' what is the full name of the burned killer who haunts teenagers in their dreams?
questions.answer: Fred or Freddy Krueger

questions.question: In the 1973 horror film 'The Exorcist,' what is the full name of the actress who played the demonically possessed young girl Regan MacNeil?
questions.answer: Linda Blair

questions.question: In the 1992 supernatural horror film 'Candyman,' what is the one-handed killer's origin story — specifically, what was done to his hand that led to his transformation into the Candyman?
questions.answer: it was sawed off with a rusty hook

questions.question: In the 1999 found-footage horror film 'The Blair Witch Project,' what do the three student filmmakers claim they are setting out to document when they enter the Black Hills Forest?
questions.answer: the legend of the Blair Witch

questions.question: In the 1982 horror film 'Poltergeist,' what is the unsettling warning that the young Carol Anne delivers to her family after staring at the static on the television screen?
questions.answer: They're here

questions.question: In the 2004 supernatural horror film 'Saw,' what is the name given to the mysterious villain who traps victims in deadly moral dilemmas, later revealed to be terminally ill civil engineer John Kramer?
questions.answer: the Jigsaw Killer

questions.question: In the 1968 psychological horror film 'Rosemary's Baby,' what disturbing truth does Rosemary ultimately uncover about her neighbors, the Castevets, and their connection to her newborn child?
questions.answer: they are devil worshippers who used her baby as the spawn of Satan

visualViability.realImageViability: medium - team logos and jerseys are trademarked by the NBA, player photos require licensing, but widely available press photos exist for editorial use
visualViability.aiGeneratedViability: medium - AI can render stylized player silhouettes, court designs, and jersey numbers but likeness of real players is legally murky
examples: Show a cropped close-up of a team logo — which franchise is this?
Show a player's silhouette mid-dunk in their jersey number — who is this?

visualViability.realImageViability: medium - runway photos and brand logos are heavily protected by fashion houses, editorial use is possible but inconsistent across brands
visualViability.aiGeneratedViability: high - AI excels at generating stylized garments, distinctive silhouettes, and brand-aesthetic illustrations without copying specific pieces
examples: Show a distinctive Chanel tweed jacket illustration — which fashion house is this associated with?
Show a runway silhouette with a red-lacquered sole — which designer's signature is this?

visualViability.realImageViability: medium - official cover art and screenshots are copyrighted by publishers, though promotional images are widely distributed and some publishers allow fan/editorial use
visualViability.aiGeneratedViability: medium - AI can render stylized game-world scenes and character archetypes but reproducing specific IP characters risks infringement
examples: Show a stylized render of a Norse mythological setting with a red-bearded warrior — which PS5 game is this?
Show a cropped game box art logo — identify this PS5 title

visualViability.realImageViability: medium - album covers are copyrighted by labels and artists, but are among the most recognizable single images in pop culture and widely reproduced in editorial contexts
visualViability.aiGeneratedViability: low - album covers are so specific and iconic that AI-generated approximations would be too vague or risk being too close to the original
examples: Show a blurred or partially obscured album cover — which artist released this?
Show a color-palette-only recreation of a famous 2000s album cover — can you identify it?

visualViability.realImageViability: high - historical paintings, maps, and pre-1928 photographs are largely in the public domain, especially for battles predating the 20th century
visualViability.aiGeneratedViability: high - AI can generate highly detailed and stylized battle scenes, period-accurate uniforms, terrain maps, and military formations with no IP concerns
examples: Show an AI-illustrated map of troop movements at a famous battle — which engagement is depicted?
Show a period painting of a naval battle scene — identify this historical conflict

visualViability.realImageViability: high - all original Jane Austen works, portraits, and 19th-century illustrations are public domain, though modern film/TV adaptations are copyrighted
visualViability.aiGeneratedViability: medium - AI can generate Regency-era scenes, character portraits, and estate illustrations but visual distinctiveness between stories is limited
examples: Show an AI illustration of a grand English country estate — which Austen novel is set here?
Show a Regency-era portrait of two women in a drawing room — which Austen title does this scene suggest?

visualViability.realImageViability: medium - car designs are trademarked and photos of specific models may require licensing, though automotive press photos are widely available for editorial use
visualViability.aiGeneratedViability: high - AI generates photorealistic and stylized car renders extremely well, and body silhouettes alone are highly recognizable without reproducing copyrighted photos
examples: Show a side-profile silhouette of an iconic supercar — identify this make and model
Show a close-up of a distinctive front grille design — which high-performance brand is this?

visualViability.realImageViability: medium - movie stills and promotional posters are copyrighted by studios, but horror iconography is among the most culturally embedded visual language in cinema
visualViability.aiGeneratedViability: high - AI is very capable of generating stylized horror scenes, iconic costume aesthetics, and atmospheric settings that evoke specific films without copying stills
examples: Show an AI-illustrated silhouette of a masked figure in a distinctive costume — which horror franchise is this?
Show a stylized haunted house illustration matching a famous film setting — identify the movie
